Lving ang
We see many things in our world
every day. All these things are living
or non-living. How are they different？
Living things
A living thing con grow and change.
A plant is a living thing, An animal is
a living thing. You are a living thing！
Living things need air, food and wate
People and animals can move.
They can get food and water from
different places. Plants cannot move
by themselves. They make their food
from sunlight, water and air.
What living things do you see
every day？

nomsliving things
Non-liwing things
A non-living thing does not need air，
food or water. A toy is a non-living thing.
A non-living thing does not grow, but
it can change. Look at the shoes. How
are they different？
A ball can move. Is it a living thing？
No, it isn't. It cannot move by itself.
It does not grow. Look around you.
What non-living things do you see？
All living and non-living things in our
world are important. We should take
care of them.

The Earth gives us important
things. We call these things
natural resources. Air, water，
wood and land are all natural
resources.
Why we need natural resources
Living things need dean air and dean
water to live. Plants need water and lano
to grow, and we need plants for food.
We also make a lot of things with wood，
like paper, chairs and houses.

How we hurt our natural resources
We should not hurt our natural resources，
We need them to live.
Smoke from cars and factories makes air
dirty. Dirty air can make people sick.
Rubbish makes the land and water dirty，
We cannot drink dirty water, and we
cannot use dirty land.
How we should take care of our
natural resources
We should all take care of the Earth's
natural resources. Here are some ideas：
• Use things longer.
• Reduce the things we use.
• Recycle our rubbish.

USA
In the USA, people watch fireworks on
New Year's Eve. On New Year's Day, many
people watch American footbal matches.

Scotland
In Scotland, people visit their family
and friends. They bring a piece of coal
for good luck.
Spain
In Spain, people wait for the
clock to ring at midnight. They
eat 12 grapes—one for each
chime.
